preview_text:
The model favours Algeria at 38–39%, giving the side a material edge over Austria's 30%. The probabilities are closely bunched, however: the draw sits at 32%, indicating a competitive fixture where no outcome commands strong consensus.

The narrow spread across all three outcomes reflects genuine uncertainty. Algeria's advantage in the model is modest—roughly 8 percentage points over Austria—and a draw remains a live outcome at nearly one-third probability. The match shape suggests a tight contest in which either team can prevail.

The model and market diverge notably. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both favour Austria at 38%, inverting the model's ranking and creating a 7–8 percentage point gap on the Algeria win probability. Both secondary sources align closely with each other on the draw (31%). This dashboard does not adjudicate between the two signals; users should weigh the divergence as material disagreement about team strength.
